file.content:
.DEFAULT_GOAL := help

DC := docker compose

help: ## Show this help
	@grep -E '^[a-zA-Z_-]+:.*?## .*$$' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | awk 'BEGIN {FS = ":.*?## "}; {printf "  \033[36m%-15s\033[0m %s\n", $$1, $$2}'

build: ## Build the workspace image
	$(DC) build

dev: ## Start the workspace container
	$(DC) up -d

shell: ## Open a shell in the workspace container
	$(DC) exec workspace bash

claude: ## Run Claude Code in the workspace container
	$(DC) exec workspace claude

codex: ## Run Codex in the workspace container
	$(DC) exec workspace codex

ab-start: ## Start agent-browser on the host
	agent-browser stream enable --port 9223

clean: ## Stop containers and remove volumes
	$(DC) down -v

.PHONY: help build dev shell claude codex ab-start clean

docker compose exec -it agent bash
```

Use `docker compose exec` (not `docker attach`). Each `exec` spawns an independent process, so multiple agents can run in parallel without stdin conflicts.

## What's Inside

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) | Anthropic's AI coding CLI |
| [Codex](https://github.com/openai/codex) | OpenAI's AI coding CLI |
| [RTK](https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk) | Token-optimized CLI proxy (60-90% token savings) |
| [mise](https://mise.jdx.dev/) | Dev tool version manager |
| [APM](https://github.com/microsoft/apm) | Agent Package Manager for MCP/skills |
| [entire](https://entire.io) | AI session capture for git |
| [git-wt](https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt) | Simplified git worktree management |
| [gh](https://cli.github.com/) | GitHub CLI |
| [ripgrep](https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep) | Fast grep (auto-used by RTK) |
| [agent-browser](https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser) | Browser automation for AI agents |
| build-essential | C/C++ compiler toolchain |

## Environment Variables

Create a `.env` file:

```
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_...
```

## Local Build

See [compose.yml](compose.yml) for a working example.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Syati/agent-stack.git
cd agent-stack
cp .env.example .env
# edit .env with your keys

make build   # build image
make dev     # start container
make shell   # open shell
make claude  # run Claude Code
make codex   # run Codex
make clean   # stop and remove
```

## agent-browser Integration

[agent-browser](https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser) is pre-installed in the container. Launch a dedicated Chrome on the host with remote debugging:

```bash
# On host: start Chrome with agent profile
/Applications/Google\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\ Chrome \
  --remote-debugging-port=9222 \
  --remote-debugging-address=0.0.0.0 \
  '--remote-allow-origins=*' \
  --user-data-dir=$HOME/.chrome-agent
```

Chrome rejects the `Host: host.docker.internal` header, so connect using the WebSocket URL via IP:

```bash
# In container
IP=$(getent hosts host.docker.internal | awk '{print $1}')
WS=$(curl -s -H "Host: localhost" http://$IP:9222/json/version | jq -r .webSocketDebuggerUrl \
  | sed "s|ws://localhost|ws://$IP:9222|")
agent-browser connect "$WS"
```

## License

MIT

toolUseResult.originalFile:
FROM debian:trixie-slim

ARG RTK_VERSION=0.42.4
ARG CODEX_VERSION=0.141.0
ARG GIT_WT_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG TARGETARCH

ENV LANG=C.UTF-8
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# System packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    ca-certificates \
    curl \
    git \
    grep \
    jq \
    locales \
    openssh-client \
    ripgrep \
    tree \
    unzip \
    && sed -i 's/^# *\(C.UTF-8\)/\1/' /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# GitHub CLI
RUN curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
      -o /usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list \
    && apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends gh \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# RTK
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) RTK_PKG="rtk_${RTK_VERSION}-1_amd64.deb" ;; \
      arm64) RTK_PKG="rtk-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk/releases/download/v${RTK_VERSION}/${RTK_PKG}" -o "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" \
    && if [ "${TARGETARCH}" = "amd64" ]; then \
         dpkg -i "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"; \
       else \
         tar xzf "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" -C /usr/local/bin; \
       fi \
    && rm -f "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"

# agent-browser
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) AB_ARCH="x64" ;; \
      arm64) AB_ARCH="arm64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser/releases/download/v${AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION}/agent-browser-linux-${AB_ARCH}" \
      -o /usr/local/bin/agent-browser \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/agent-browser

# git-wt
RUN curl -fsSL "https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt/releases/download/v${GIT_WT_VERSION}/git-wt_${GIT_WT_VERSION}-1_${TARGETARCH}.deb" \
      -o /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && dpkg -i /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && rm /tmp/git-wt.deb

# Non-root user
RUN useradd -m -s /bin/bash agent
RUN echo 'eval "$(git wt --init bash)"' >> /home/agent/.bashrc
USER agent
WORKDIR /home/agent

# mise
RUN curl -fsSL https://mise.run | sh
ENV PATH="/home/agent/.local/bin:${PATH}"

# Claude Code
RUN curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash

# entire (AI session capture for git)
RUN curl -fsSL https://entire.io/install.sh | bash

# Codex
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) CODEX_ARCH="x86_64" ;; \
      arm64) CODEX_ARCH="aarch64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/openai/codex/releases/download/rust-v${CODEX_VERSION}/codex-${CODEX_ARCH}-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz" \
      -o /tmp/codex.tar.gz \
    && tar xzf /tmp/codex.tar.gz -C /home/agent/.local/bin \
    && mv /home/agent/.local/bin/codex-*-unknown-linux-musl /home/agent/.local/bin/codex \
    && rm /tmp/codex.tar.gz

# APM (requires python3)
USER root
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3 python3-pip \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
USER agent
RUN pip3 install --user --break-system-packages apm-cli

WORKDIR /workspace
CMD ["sleep", "infinity"]

file.content:
.DEFAULT_GOAL := help

DC := docker compose

help: ## Show this help
	@grep -E '^[a-zA-Z_-]+:.*?## .*$$' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | awk 'BEGIN {FS = ":.*?## "}; {printf "  \033[36m%-15s\033[0m %s\n", $$1, $$2}'

build: ## Build the workspace image
	$(DC) build

dev: ## Start the workspace container
	$(DC) up -d

shell: ## Open a shell in the workspace container
	$(DC) exec workspace bash

claude: ## Run Claude Code in the workspace container
	$(DC) exec workspace claude

codex: ## Run Codex in the workspace container
	$(DC) exec workspace codex

chrome: ## Start Chrome with remote debugging for agent-browser
	/Applications/Google\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\ Chrome \
		--remote-debugging-port=9222 \
		--remote-debugging-address=0.0.0.0 \
		--user-data-dir=$(HOME)/.chrome-agent

ab-connect: ## Connect agent-browser to host Chrome (run inside container)
	@IP=$$(getent hosts host.docker.internal | awk '{print $$1}') && \
	WS=$$(curl -s -H "Host: localhost" http://$$IP:9222/json/version | jq -r .webSocketDebuggerUrl \
		| sed "s|ws://localhost|ws://$$IP:9222|") && \
	echo "Connecting to host Chrome at $$WS"

clean: ## Stop containers and remove volumes
	$(DC) down -v

.PHONY: help build dev shell claude codex chrome ab-connect clean

message.content: entrypoint じゃなくて dockerfile でいいの？ あと port 9222 ってユーザー次第で変わらない？

content.text:
どちらもその通りです。

- **bashrc vs entrypoint**: bashrc は interactive shell でしか読まれないので、`claude` や `codex` が内部で起動する non-interactive shell では `CHROME_CDP_URL` が見えません。ただ entrypoint も先ほど不要としたので、Dockerfile の `ENV` で設定するのが正解ですが、`getent` はビルド時には使えない（コンテナ起動時にしか `host.docker.internal` が解決できない）ので、entrypoint が必要です。
- **port**: ユーザーが変えられるようにすべきですね。

entrypoint を作り直して、ポートは環境変数で設定可能にしましょう。

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

HOST_IP=$(getent hosts host.docker.internal 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}')
if [ -n "$HOST_IP" ]; then
  export CHROME_CDP_URL="http://${HOST_IP}:${CHROME_REMOTE_PORT:-9222}"
fi

exec "$@"

    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser/releases/download/v${AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION}/agent-browser-linux-${AB_ARCH}" \
      -o /usr/local/bin/agent-browser \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/agent-browser

# git-wt
RUN curl -fsSL "https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt/releases/download/v${GIT_WT_VERSION}/git-wt_${GIT_WT_VERSION}-1_${TARGETARCH}.deb" \
      -o /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && dpkg -i /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && rm /tmp/git-wt.deb

# Non-root user
RUN useradd -m -s /bin/bash agent
RUN echo 'eval "$(git wt --init bash)"' >> /home/agent/.bashrc \
    && echo 'HOST_IP=$(getent hosts host.docker.internal | awk "{print \$1}") && export CHROME_CDP_URL="http://${HOST_IP}:9222"' >> /home/agent/.bashrc
USER agent
WORKDIR /home/agent

# mise
RUN curl -fsSL https://mise.run | sh
ENV PATH="/home/agent/.local/bin:${PATH}"

# Claude Code
RUN curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash

# entire (AI session capture for git)
RUN curl -fsSL https://entire.io/install.sh | bash

# Codex
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) CODEX_ARCH="x86_64" ;; \
      arm64) CODEX_ARCH="aarch64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/openai/codex/releases/download/rust-v${CODEX_VERSION}/codex-${CODEX_ARCH}-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz" \
      -o /tmp/codex.tar.gz \
    && tar xzf /tmp/codex.tar.gz -C /home/agent/.local/bin \
    && mv /home/agent/.local/bin/codex-*-unknown-linux-musl /home/agent/.local/bin/codex \
    && rm /tmp/codex.tar.gz

# APM (requires python3)
USER root
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3 python3-pip \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
USER agent
RUN pip3 install --user --break-system-packages apm-cli

WORKDIR /workspace
CMD ["sleep", "infinity"]

toolUseResult.originalFile:
FROM debian:trixie-slim

ARG RTK_VERSION=0.42.4
ARG CODEX_VERSION=0.141.0
ARG GIT_WT_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG TARGETARCH

ENV LANG=C.UTF-8
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# System packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    ca-certificates \
    curl \
    git \
    grep \
    jq \
    locales \
    openssh-client \
    ripgrep \
    tree \
    unzip \
    && sed -i 's/^# *\(C.UTF-8\)/\1/' /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# GitHub CLI
RUN curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
      -o /usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list \
    && apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends gh \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# RTK
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) RTK_PKG="rtk_${RTK_VERSION}-1_amd64.deb" ;; \
      arm64) RTK_PKG="rtk-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk/releases/download/v${RTK_VERSION}/${RTK_PKG}" -o "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" \
    && if [ "${TARGETARCH}" = "amd64" ]; then \
         dpkg -i "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"; \
       else \
         tar xzf "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" -C /usr/local/bin; \
       fi \
    && rm -f "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"

# agent-browser
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) AB_ARCH="x64" ;; \
      arm64) AB_ARCH="arm64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser/releases/download/v${AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION}/agent-browser-linux-${AB_ARCH}" \
      -o /usr/local/bin/agent-browser \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/agent-browser

# git-wt
RUN curl -fsSL "https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt/releases/download/v${GIT_WT_VERSION}/git-wt_${GIT_WT_VERSION}-1_${TARGETARCH}.deb" \
      -o /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && dpkg -i /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && rm /tmp/git-wt.deb

# Non-root user
RUN useradd -m -s /bin/bash agent
RUN echo 'eval "$(git wt --init bash)"' >> /home/agent/.bashrc
COPY docker/entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh
USER agent
WORKDIR /home/agent

# mise
RUN curl -fsSL https://mise.run | sh
ENV PATH="/home/agent/.local/bin:${PATH}"

# Claude Code
RUN curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash

# entire (AI session capture for git)
RUN curl -fsSL https://entire.io/install.sh | bash

# Codex
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) CODEX_ARCH="x86_64" ;; \
      arm64) CODEX_ARCH="aarch64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/openai/codex/releases/download/rust-v${CODEX_VERSION}/codex-${CODEX_ARCH}-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz" \
      -o /tmp/codex.tar.gz \
    && tar xzf /tmp/codex.tar.gz -C /home/agent/.local/bin \
    && mv /home/agent/.local/bin/codex-*-unknown-linux-musl /home/agent/.local/bin/codex \
    && rm /tmp/codex.tar.gz

# APM (requires python3)
USER root
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3 python3-pip \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
USER agent
RUN pip3 install --user --break-system-packages apm-cli

WORKDIR /workspace
CMD ["sleep", "infinity"]

file.content:
.DEFAULT_GOAL := help

DC := docker compose

help: ## Show this help
	@grep -E '^[a-zA-Z_-]+:.*?## .*$$' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | awk 'BEGIN {FS = ":.*?## "}; {printf "  \033[36m%-15s\033[0m %s\n", $$1, $$2}'

build: ## Build the workspace image
	$(DC) build

dev: ## Start the workspace container
	$(DC) up -d

shell: ## Open a shell in the workspace container
	$(DC) exec workspace bash

claude: ## Run Claude Code in the workspace container
	$(DC) exec workspace claude

codex: ## Run Codex in the workspace container
	$(DC) exec workspace codex

chrome: ## Start Chrome with remote debugging for agent-browser
	/Applications/Google\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\ Chrome \
		--remote-debugging-port=9222 \
		--remote-debugging-address=0.0.0.0 \
		--user-data-dir=$(HOME)/.chrome-agent

ab-connect: ## Connect agent-browser to host Chrome (run inside container)
	@WS=$$(curl -s -H "Host: localhost" $$CHROME_CDP_URL/json/version | jq -r .webSocketDebuggerUrl \
		| sed "s|ws://localhost|ws://$${CHROME_CDP_URL#http://}|") && \
	agent-browser connect "$$WS"

clean: ## Stop containers and remove volumes
	$(DC) down -v

.PHONY: help build dev shell claude codex chrome ab-connect clean

content.text: `.env` の `CHROME_REMOTE_PORT` を Makefile と entrypoint の両方で参照するようにしました。未設定時は 9222 がデフォルトです。

message.content: -include .env で読み込めるの？

content.text:
はい、Makefile の `-include` は指定ファイルを読み込んで変数として展開します。`.env` の `KEY=VALUE` 形式は Makefile の変数定義と互換性があるのでそのまま使えます。`-` prefix はファイルが無くてもエラーにしません。

ただし `.env` にシェル構文（`export`、クォート、変数展開 `${}` など）が含まれると壊れます。今の `.env.example` の形式なら問題ないです。

Make CHROME_REMOTE_PORT configurable via .env

for both host Chrome launch and container entrypoint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push

README の Quick Start に Chrome の起動コマンドも書いておくのがシンプルですね。Makefile の `chrome` ターゲットはこのリポジトリのローカルビルド用として残す形で。

file.content:
# agent-stack

AI agent development container with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code), [Codex](https://github.com/openai/codex), [RTK](https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk), [mise](https://mise.jdx.dev/), [APM](https://github.com/microsoft/apm), [entire](https://entire.io), and [git-wt](https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt) pre-installed.

## Quick Start

Add to your project's `compose.yml`:

```yaml
services:
  agent:
    image: ghcr.io/syati/agent-stack:latest
    volumes:
      - .:/workspace
    env_file:
      - .env
    extra_hosts:
      - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway"
```

Start the container and connect:

```bash
docker compose up -d
docker compose exec -it agent bash
```

Use `docker compose exec` (not `docker attach`). Each `exec` spawns an independent process, so multiple agents can run in parallel without stdin conflicts.

## What's Inside

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) | Anthropic's AI coding CLI |
| [Codex](https://github.com/openai/codex) | OpenAI's AI coding CLI |
| [RTK](https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk) | Token-optimized CLI proxy (60-90% token savings) |
| [mise](https://mise.jdx.dev/) | Dev tool version manager |
| [APM](https://github.com/microsoft/apm) | Agent Package Manager for MCP/skills |
| [entire](https://entire.io) | AI session capture for git |
| [git-wt](https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt) | Simplified git worktree management |
| [gh](https://cli.github.com/) | GitHub CLI |
| [ripgrep](https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep) | Fast grep (auto-used by RTK) |
| [agent-browser](https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser) | Browser automation for AI agents |
| build-essential | C/C++ compiler toolchain |

## Environment Variables

Create a `.env` file:

```
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_...
```

## Local Build

See [compose.yml](compose.yml) for a working example.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Syati/agent-stack.git
cd agent-stack
cp .env.example .env
# edit .env with your keys

make build   # build image
make dev     # start container
make shell   # open shell
make claude  # run Claude Code
make codex   # run Codex
make clean   # stop and remove
```

## agent-browser Integration

[agent-browser](https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser) is pre-installed in the container. Connect to Chrome on the host via remote debugging:

```bash
# On host: start Chrome with agent profile
make chrome

# In container: connect to host Chrome
make ab-connect
```

## License

MIT

```bash

make chrome

# In container: connect to host Chrome
make ab-connect
```
input.new_string:
## agent-browser Integration

[agent-browser](https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser) is pre-installed in the container. Start Chrome on the host with remote debugging, then connect from the container.

**Host side** (start Chrome with a dedicated profile):

```bash
/Applications/Google\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\ Chrome \
  --remote-debugging-port=9222 \
  --remote-debugging-address=0.0.0.0 \
  '--remote-allow-origins=*' \
  --user-data-dir=$HOME/.chrome-agent
```

**Container side** (`CHROME_CDP_URL` is set automatically by the entrypoint):

```bash
WS=$(curl -s -H "Host: localhost" $CHROME_CDP_URL/json/version | jq -r .webSocketDebuggerUrl \
  | sed "s|ws://localhost|ws://${CHROME_CDP_URL#http://}|")
agent-browser connect "$WS"
```

Port is configurable via `CHROME_REMOTE_PORT` in `.env` (default: 9222).

# agent-stack

AI agent development container with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code), [Codex](https://github.com/openai/codex), [RTK](https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk), [mise](https://mise.jdx.dev/), [APM](https://github.com/microsoft/apm), [entire](https://entire.io), and [git-wt](https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt) pre-installed.

## Quick Start

Add to your project's `compose.yml`:

```yaml
services:
  agent:
    image: ghcr.io/syati/agent-stack:latest
    volumes:
      - .:/workspace
    env_file:
      - .env
    extra_hosts:
      - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway"
```

Start the container and connect:

```bash
docker compose up -d
docker compose exec -it agent bash
```

Use `docker compose exec` (not `docker attach`). Each `exec` spawns an independent process, so multiple agents can run in parallel without stdin conflicts.

## What's Inside

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) | Anthropic's AI coding CLI |
| [Codex](https://github.com/openai/codex) | OpenAI's AI coding CLI |
| [RTK](https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk) | Token-optimized CLI proxy (60-90% token savings) |
| [mise](https://mise.jdx.dev/) | Dev tool version manager |
| [APM](https://github.com/microsoft/apm) | Agent Package Manager for MCP/skills |
| [entire](https://entire.io) | AI session capture for git |
| [git-wt](https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt) | Simplified git worktree management |
| [gh](https://cli.github.com/) | GitHub CLI |
| [ripgrep](https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep) | Fast grep (auto-used by RTK) |
| [agent-browser](https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser) | Browser automation for AI agents |
| build-essential | C/C++ compiler toolchain |

## Environment Variables

Create a `.env` file:

```
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_...
```

## Local Build

See [compose.yml](compose.yml) for a working example.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Syati/agent-stack.git
cd agent-stack
cp .env.example .env
# edit .env with your keys

make build   # build image
make dev     # start container
make shell   # open shell
make claude  # run Claude Code
make codex   # run Codex
make clean   # stop and remove
```

## agent-browser Integration

[agent-browser](https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser) is pre-installed in the container. Start Chrome on the host with remote debugging, then connect from the container.

**Host side** (start Chrome with a dedicated profile):

```bash
/Applications/Google\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\ Chrome \
  --remote-debugging-port=9222 \
  --remote-debugging-address=0.0.0.0 \
  '--remote-allow-origins=*' \
  --user-data-dir=$HOME/.chrome-agent
```

**Container side** (`CHROME_CDP_URL` is set automatically by the entrypoint):

```bash
WS=$(curl -s -H "Host: localhost" $CHROME_CDP_URL/json/version | jq -r .webSocketDebuggerUrl \
  | sed "s|ws://localhost|ws://${CHROME_CDP_URL#http://}|")
agent-browser connect "$WS"
```

Port is configurable via `CHROME_REMOTE_PORT` in `.env` (default: 9222).

## License

MIT

content.text:
Chrome が起動していないですね。ホスト側で Chrome を立ち上げてもらう必要があります。ただ `agent-browser connect $CHROME_CDP_URL` の形でいけるか確認したいので、Host ヘッダーの問題が出るか試すには Chrome が必要です。

結論としては、`agent-browser connect` が内部で `/json/version` を叩くときに `Host: <IP>` ヘッダーを送るので Chrome に拒否されます。なのでワークアラウンドの curl + sed が必要になっています。

ただ、これは entrypoint 側で解決すべきですね。`CHROME_CDP_URL` だけで使えるように、entrypoint で WebSocket URL まで解決しておきましょう。

content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
2	set -e
3	
4	HOST_IP=$(getent hosts host.docker.internal 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}')
5	if [ -n "$HOST_IP" ]; then
6	  export CHROME_CDP_URL="http://${HOST_IP}:${CHROME_REMOTE_PORT:-9222}"
7	fi
8	
9	exec "$@"
10

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

HOST_IP=$(getent hosts host.docker.internal 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}')
if [ -n "$HOST_IP" ]; then
  CHROME_PORT="${CHROME_REMOTE_PORT:-9222}"
  export CHROME_CDP_URL="http://${HOST_IP}:${CHROME_PORT}"

  WS_URL=$(curl -sf -H "Host: localhost" "http://${HOST_IP}:${CHROME_PORT}/json/version" 2>/dev/null \
    | jq -r .webSocketDebuggerUrl 2>/dev/null \
    | sed "s|ws://localhost|ws://${HOST_IP}:${CHROME_PORT}|")
  if [ -n "$WS_URL" ] && [ "$WS_URL" != "null" ]; then
    export CHROME_WS_URL="$WS_URL"
  fi
fi

exec "$@"

shell: ## Open a shell in the workspace container

content.text:
`-include .env` で `.env` の内容が `MAKEFILE_LIST` に追加され、`.env` のファイル名が出力に混ざっています。RTK のフィルタで `Makefile` だけに見えていますが、実際は `.env` のパースが壊しています。

`MAKEFILE_LIST` の代わりに `Makefile` を直接指定すれば解決します。

make chrome したときエラーがでるけどなんだろ？
Trying to load the allocator multiple times. This is *not* supported.
[25705:36560419:0621/141204.458434:ERROR:google_apis/gcm/engine/registration_request.cc:291] Registration response error message: DEPRECATED_ENDPOINT

content.text:
Chrome 自体の内部エラーで、動作には影響しません。GCM（Google Cloud Messaging）の deprecated API を呼ぼうとして失敗しているだけです。

`--user-data-dir` で新しいプロファイルを使っているので、Chrome が初回セットアップ時にプッシュ通知の登録を試みて失敗しています。無視して大丈夫です。

pri#!/bin/bash
set -e

HOST_IP=$(getent hosts host.docker.internal 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}')
if [ -n "$HOST_IP" ]; then
  CHROME_PORT="${CHROME_REMOTE_PORT:-9222}"
  export CHROME_CDP_URL="http://${HOST_IP}:${CHROME_PORT}"

  WS_URL=$(curl -sf -H "Host: localhost" "http://${HOST_IP}:${CHROME_PORT}/json/version" 2>/dev/null \
    | jq -r .webSocketDebuggerUrl 2>/dev/null \
    | sed "s|ws://localhost|ws://${HOST_IP}:${CHROME_PORT}|")
  if [ -n "$WS_URL" ] && [ "$WS_URL" != "null" ]; then
    export CHROME_WS_URL="$WS_URL"
  fi
fi

exec "$@"

content.text:
entrypoint は PID 1 (`sleep infinity`) の起動時に実行されますが、`docker compose exec` は新しいプロセスなので entrypoint を経由しません。つまり `exec` で入ったシェルには `CHROME_CDP_URL` が見えません。

解決策は、entrypoint ではなく Dockerfile の `ENV` で設定するか、bashrc に戻すかですが、ビルド時には `host.docker.internal` が解決できないので bashrc が正解でした。

  if [ -n "$WS_URL" ] && [ "$WS_URL" != "null" ]; then
    export CHROME_WS_URL="$WS_URL"
  fi
fi

exec "$@"

FROM debian:trixie-slim

ARG RTK_VERSION=0.42.4
ARG CODEX_VERSION=0.141.0
ARG GIT_WT_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG TARGETARCH

ENV LANG=C.UTF-8
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# System packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    ca-certificates \
    curl \
    git \
    grep \
    jq \
    locales \
    openssh-client \
    ripgrep \
    tree \
    unzip \
    && sed -i 's/^# *\(C.UTF-8\)/\1/' /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# GitHub CLI
RUN curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
      -o /usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list \
    && apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends gh \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# RTK
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) RTK_PKG="rtk_${RTK_VERSION}-1_amd64.deb" ;; \
      arm64) RTK_PKG="rtk-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk/releases/download/v${RTK_VERSION}/${RTK_PKG}" -o "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" \
    && if [ "${TARGETARCH}" = "amd64" ]; then \
         dpkg -i "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"; \
       else \
         tar xzf "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" -C /usr/local/bin; \
       fi \
    && rm -f "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"

# agent-browser
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) AB_ARCH="x64" ;; \
      arm64) AB_ARCH="arm64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser/releases/download/v${AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION}/agent-browser-linux-${AB_ARCH}" \
      -o /usr/local/bin/agent-browser \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/agent-browser

# git-wt
RUN curl -fsSL "https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt/releases/download/v${GIT_WT_VERSION}/git-wt_${GIT_WT_VERSION}-1_${TARGETARCH}.deb" \
      -o /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && dpkg -i /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && rm /tmp/git-wt.deb

# Non-root user
RUN useradd -m -s /bin/bash agent
RUN echo 'eval "$(git wt --init bash)"' >> /home/agent/.bashrc
COPY docker/entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh
USER agent
WORKDIR /home/agent

# mise
RUN curl -fsSL https://mise.run | sh
ENV PATH="/home/agent/.local/bin:${PATH}"

# Claude Code
RUN curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash

# entire (AI session capture for git)
RUN curl -fsSL https://entire.io/install.sh | bash

# Codex
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) CODEX_ARCH="x86_64" ;; \
      arm64) CODEX_ARCH="aarch64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/openai/codex/releases/download/rust-v${CODEX_VERSION}/codex-${CODEX_ARCH}-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz" \
      -o /tmp/codex.tar.gz \
    && tar xzf /tmp/codex.tar.gz -C /home/agent/.local/bin \
    && mv /home/agent/.local/bin/codex-*-unknown-linux-musl /home/agent/.local/bin/codex \
    && rm /tmp/codex.tar.gz

# APM (requires python3)
USER root
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3 python3-pip \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
USER agent
RUN pip3 install --user --break-system-packages apm-cli

WORKDIR /workspace
ENTRYPOINT ["entrypoint.sh"]
CMD ["sleep", "infinity"]

確かに、echo で複数行を bashrc に書き込むのは読みにくいですし壊れやすいです。

もっと良い方法: bashrc 用のスクリプトをファイルとして COPY して、bashrc から source するのがクリーンです。

FROM debian:trixie-slim

ARG RTK_VERSION=0.42.4
ARG CODEX_VERSION=0.141.0
ARG GIT_WT_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG TARGETARCH

ENV LANG=C.UTF-8
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# System packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    ca-certificates \
    curl \
    git \
    grep \
    jq \
    locales \
    openssh-client \
    ripgrep \
    tree \
    unzip \
    && sed -i 's/^# *\(C.UTF-8\)/\1/' /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# GitHub CLI
RUN curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
      -o /usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list \
    && apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends gh \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# RTK
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) RTK_PKG="rtk_${RTK_VERSION}-1_amd64.deb" ;; \
      arm64) RTK_PKG="rtk-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk/releases/download/v${RTK_VERSION}/${RTK_PKG}" -o "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" \
    && if [ "${TARGETARCH}" = "amd64" ]; then \
         dpkg -i "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"; \
       else \
         tar xzf "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" -C /usr/local/bin; \
       fi \
    && rm -f "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"

# agent-browser
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) AB_ARCH="x64" ;; \
      arm64) AB_ARCH="arm64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser/releases/download/v${AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION}/agent-browser-linux-${AB_ARCH}" \
      -o /usr/local/bin/agent-browser \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/agent-browser

# git-wt
RUN curl -fsSL "https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt/releases/download/v${GIT_WT_VERSION}/git-wt_${GIT_WT_VERSION}-1_${TARGETARCH}.deb" \
      -o /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && dpkg -i /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && rm /tmp/git-wt.deb

# Non-root user
RUN useradd -m -s /bin/bash agent
COPY docker/profile.sh /etc/profile.d/agent-stack.sh
RUN echo '. /etc/profile.d/agent-stack.sh' >> /home/agent/.bashrc
COPY docker/entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh
USER agent
WORKDIR /home/agent

# mise
RUN curl -fsSL https://mise.run | sh
ENV PATH="/home/agent/.local/bin:${PATH}"

# Claude Code
RUN curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash

# entire (AI session capture for git)
RUN curl -fsSL https://entire.io/install.sh | bash

# Codex
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) CODEX_ARCH="x86_64" ;; \
      arm64) CODEX_ARCH="aarch64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/openai/codex/releases/download/rust-v${CODEX_VERSION}/codex-${CODEX_ARCH}-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz" \
      -o /tmp/codex.tar.gz \
    && tar xzf /tmp/codex.tar.gz -C /home/agent/.local/bin \
    && mv /home/agent/.local/bin/codex-*-unknown-linux-musl /home/agent/.local/bin/codex \
    && rm /tmp/codex.tar.gz

# APM (requires python3)
USER root
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3 python3-pip \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
USER agent
RUN pip3 install --user --break-system-packages apm-cli

WORKDIR /workspace
ENTRYPOINT ["entrypoint.sh"]
CMD ["sleep", "infinity"]

message.content: echo profile.sh したのち、profile.sh って不要じゃない？

content.text:
いえ、`profile.sh` はファイルとして残す方がいいです。先ほど echo で bashrc に書き込むのが微妙という話になったので、設定内容を `docker/profile.sh` に独立ファイルとして管理し、COPY してから bashrc で source する方式にしました。

もし echo で直接書くのに戻すなら `docker/profile.sh` は不要ですが、それだとまた読みにくくなります。今の方式がクリーンだと思います。

aiTitle: AI Docker container with MCP and agent-browser setup

content.text:
はい。COPY で `docker/profile.sh` をコンテナ内の `~/.profile.sh` に配置して、bashrc の最後に `. ~/.profile.sh` を追加しているので、シェル起動時に source されます。

設定内容の編集は `docker/profile.sh` だけで済みます。

28			--remote-debugging-address=0.0.0.0 \
29			--user-data-dir=$(HOME)/.chrome-agent
30

32		agent-browser connect "$$CHROME_WS_URL"

36

file.content:
.DEFAULT_GOAL := help

DC := docker compose
-include .env
CHROME_REMOTE_PORT ?= 9222

eval "$(git wt --init bash)"

HOST_IP=$(getent hosts host.docker.internal 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}')
if [ -n "$HOST_IP" ]; then
  export CHROME_CDP_URL="http://${HOST_IP}:${CHROME_REMOTE_PORT:-9222}"
  WS_URL=$(curl -sf -H "Host: localhost" "$CHROME_CDP_URL/json/version" 2>/dev/null \
    | jq -r .webSocketDebuggerUrl 2>/dev/null \
    | sed "s|ws://localhost|ws://${HOST_IP}:${CHROME_REMOTE_PORT:-9222}|")
  [ -n "$WS_URL" ] && [ "$WS_URL" != "null" ] && export CHROME_WS_URL="$WS_URL"
fi

input.content:
eval "$(git wt --init bash)"

HOST_IP=$(getent hosts host.docker.internal 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}')
if [ -n "$HOST_IP" ]; then
  export CHROME_CDP_URL="http://${HOST_IP}:${CHROME_REMOTE_PORT:-9222}"
fi

chrome-connect() {
  if [ -z "$CHROME_CDP_URL" ]; then
    echo "CHROME_CDP_URL is not set" >&2
    return 1
  fi
  local ip="${CHROME_CDP_URL#http://}"
  local ws_url
  ws_url=$(curl -sf -H "Host: localhost" "$CHROME_CDP_URL/json/version" \
    | jq -r .webSocketDebuggerUrl \
    | sed "s|ws://localhost|ws://${ip}|")
  if [ -n "$ws_url" ] && [ "$ws_url" != "null" ]; then
    export CHROME_WS_URL="$ws_url"
    echo "CHROME_WS_URL=$CHROME_WS_URL"
  else
    echo "Failed to connect to Chrome at $CHROME_CDP_URL" >&2
    return 1
  fi
}

file.content:
```

## agent-browser Integration

[agent-browser](https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser) is pre-installed in the container. Start Chrome on the host with remote debugging, then connect from the container.

**Host side** (start Chrome with a dedicated profile):

```bash
/Applications/Google\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\ Chrome \
  --remote-debugging-port=9222 \
  --remote-debugging-address=0.0.0.0 \
  --user-data-dir=$HOME/.chrome-agent
```

**Container side** (`CHROME_WS_URL` is set automatically by the entrypoint when Chrome is running):

```bash
agent-browser connect "$CHROME_WS_URL"
```

Port is configurable via `CHROME_REMOTE_PORT` in `.env` (default: 9222).

## License

```

```bash

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# agent-stack

AI agent development container with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code), [Codex](https://github.com/openai/codex), [RTK](https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk), [mise](https://mise.jdx.dev/), [APM](https://github.com/microsoft/apm), [entire](https://entire.io), and [git-wt](https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt) pre-installed.

## Quick Start

Add to your project's `compose.yml`:

```yaml
services:
  agent:
    image: ghcr.io/syati/agent-stack:latest
    volumes:
      - .:/workspace
    env_file:
      - .env
    extra_hosts:
      - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway"
```

Start the container and connect:

```bash
docker compose up -d
docker compose exec -it agent bash
```

Use `docker compose exec` (not `docker attach`). Each `exec` spawns an independent process, so multiple agents can run in parallel without stdin conflicts.

## What's Inside

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) | Anthropic's AI coding CLI |
| [Codex](https://github.com/openai/codex) | OpenAI's AI coding CLI |
| [RTK](https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk) | Token-optimized CLI proxy (60-90% token savings) |
| [mise](https://mise.jdx.dev/) | Dev tool version manager |
| [APM](https://github.com/microsoft/apm) | Agent Package Manager for MCP/skills |
| [entire](https://entire.io) | AI session capture for git |
| [git-wt](https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt) | Simplified git worktree management |
| [gh](https://cli.github.com/) | GitHub CLI |
| [ripgrep](https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep) | Fast grep (auto-used by RTK) |
| [agent-browser](https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser) | Browser automation for AI agents |
| build-essential | C/C++ compiler toolchain |

## Environment Variables

Create a `.env` file:

```
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_...
```

## Local Build

See [compose.yml](compose.yml) for a working example.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Syati/agent-stack.git
cd agent-stack
cp .env.example .env
# edit .env with your keys

make build   # build image
make dev     # start container
make shell   # open shell
make claude  # run Claude Code
make codex   # run Codex
make clean   # stop and remove
```

## agent-browser Integration

[agent-browser](https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser) is pre-installed in the container. Start Chrome on the host with remote debugging, then connect from the container.

**Host side** (start Chrome with a dedicated profile):

```bash
/Applications/Google\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\ Chrome \
  --remote-debugging-port=9222 \
  --remote-debugging-address=0.0.0.0 \
  --user-data-dir=$HOME/.chrome-agent
```

**Container side** (`CHROME_WS_URL` is set automatically by the entrypoint when Chrome is running):

```bash
agent-browser connect "$CHROME_WS_URL"
```

Port is configurable via `CHROME_REMOTE_PORT` in `.env` (default: 9222).

## License

MIT

# agent-stack

AI agent development container with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code), [Codex](https://github.com/openai/codex), [RTK](https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk), [mise](https://mise.jdx.dev/), [APM](https://github.com/microsoft/apm), [entire](https://entire.io), and [git-wt](https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt) pre-installed.

## Quick Start

Add to your project's `compose.yml`:

```yaml
services:
  agent:
    image: ghcr.io/syati/agent-stack:latest
    volumes:
      - .:/workspace
    env_file:
      - .env
    extra_hosts:
      - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway"
```

Start the container and connect:

```bash
docker compose up -d
docker compose exec -it agent bash
```

Use `docker compose exec` (not `docker attach`). Each `exec` spawns an independent process, so multiple agents can run in parallel without stdin conflicts.

## What's Inside

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) | Anthropic's AI coding CLI |
| [Codex](https://github.com/openai/codex) | OpenAI's AI coding CLI |
| [RTK](https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk) | Token-optimized CLI proxy (60-90% token savings) |
| [mise](https://mise.jdx.dev/) | Dev tool version manager |
| [APM](https://github.com/microsoft/apm) | Agent Package Manager for MCP/skills |
| [entire](https://entire.io) | AI session capture for git |
| [git-wt](https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt) | Simplified git worktree management |
| [gh](https://cli.github.com/) | GitHub CLI |
| [ripgrep](https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep) | Fast grep (auto-used by RTK) |
| [agent-browser](https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser) | Browser automation for AI agents |
| build-essential | C/C++ compiler toolchain |

## Environment Variables

Create a `.env` file:

```
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_...
```

## Local Build

See [compose.yml](compose.yml) for a working example.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Syati/agent-stack.git
cd agent-stack
cp .env.example .env
# edit .env with your keys

make build   # build image
make dev     # start container
make shell   # open shell
make claude  # run Claude Code
make codex   # run Codex
make clean   # stop and remove
```

## agent-browser Integration

[agent-browser](https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser) is pre-installed in the container. Start Chrome on the host with remote debugging, then connect from the container.

**Host side** (start Chrome with a dedicated profile):

```bash
/Applications/Google\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\ Chrome \
  --remote-debugging-port=9222 \
  --remote-debugging-address=0.0.0.0 \
  --user-data-dir=$HOME/.chrome-agent
```

**Container side** (run `chrome-connect` to resolve WebSocket URL and connect):

```bash
chrome-connect
```

Port is configurable via `CHROME_REMOTE_PORT` in `.env` (default: 9222).

## License

MIT

toolUseResult.originalFile:
FROM debian:trixie-slim

ARG RTK_VERSION=0.42.4
ARG CODEX_VERSION=0.141.0
ARG GIT_WT_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG TARGETARCH

ENV LANG=C.UTF-8
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# System packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    ca-certificates \
    curl \
    git \
    grep \
    jq \
    locales \
    openssh-client \
    ripgrep \
    tree \
    unzip \
    && sed -i 's/^# *\(C.UTF-8\)/\1/' /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# GitHub CLI
RUN curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
      -o /usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list \
    && apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends gh \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# RTK
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) RTK_PKG="rtk_${RTK_VERSION}-1_amd64.deb" ;; \
      arm64) RTK_PKG="rtk-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk/releases/download/v${RTK_VERSION}/${RTK_PKG}" -o "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" \
    && if [ "${TARGETARCH}" = "amd64" ]; then \
         dpkg -i "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"; \
       else \
         tar xzf "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" -C /usr/local/bin; \
       fi \
    && rm -f "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"

# agent-browser
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) AB_ARCH="x64" ;; \
      arm64) AB_ARCH="arm64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser/releases/download/v${AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION}/agent-browser-linux-${AB_ARCH}" \
      -o /usr/local/bin/agent-browser \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/agent-browser

# git-wt
RUN curl -fsSL "https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt/releases/download/v${GIT_WT_VERSION}/git-wt_${GIT_WT_VERSION}-1_${TARGETARCH}.deb" \
      -o /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && dpkg -i /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && rm /tmp/git-wt.deb

# Non-root user
RUN useradd -m -s /bin/bash agent
COPY docker/profile.sh /home/agent/.profile.sh
RUN echo '. ~/.profile.sh' >> /home/agent/.bashrc
USER agent
WORKDIR /home/agent

# mise
RUN curl -fsSL https://mise.run | sh
ENV PATH="/home/agent/.local/bin:${PATH}"

# Claude Code
RUN curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash

# entire (AI session capture for git)
RUN curl -fsSL https://entire.io/install.sh | bash

# Codex
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) CODEX_ARCH="x86_64" ;; \
      arm64) CODEX_ARCH="aarch64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/openai/codex/releases/download/rust-v${CODEX_VERSION}/codex-${CODEX_ARCH}-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz" \
      -o /tmp/codex.tar.gz \
    && tar xzf /tmp/codex.tar.gz -C /home/agent/.local/bin \
    && mv /home/agent/.local/bin/codex-*-unknown-linux-musl /home/agent/.local/bin/codex \
    && rm /tmp/codex.tar.gz

# APM (requires python3)
USER root
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3 python3-pip \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
USER agent
RUN pip3 install --user --break-system-packages apm-cli

WORKDIR /workspace
CMD ["sleep", "infinity"]

FROM debian:trixie-slim

ARG RTK_VERSION=0.42.4
ARG CODEX_VERSION=0.141.0
ARG GIT_WT_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG TARGETARCH

ENV LANG=C.UTF-8
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# System packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    ca-certificates \
    curl \
    git \
    grep \
    jq \
    locales \
    openssh-client \
    ripgrep \

    unzip \

    && sed -i 's/^# *\(C.UTF-8\)/\1/' /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# GitHub CLI
RUN curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
      -o /usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list \
    && apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends gh \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# RTK
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) RTK_PKG="rtk_${RTK_VERSION}-1_amd64.deb" ;; \
      arm64) RTK_PKG="rtk-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk/releases/download/v${RTK_VERSION}/${RTK_PKG}" -o "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" \
    && if [ "${TARGETARCH}" = "amd64" ]; then \
         dpkg -i "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"; \
       else \
         tar xzf "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" -C /usr/local/bin; \
       fi \
    && rm -f "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"

# agent-browser
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) AB_ARCH="x64" ;; \
      arm64) AB_ARCH="arm64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser/releases/download/v${AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION}/agent-browser-linux-${AB_ARCH}" \
      -o /usr/local/bin/agent-browser \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/agent-browser

# git-wt
RUN curl -fsSL "https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt/releases/download/v${GIT_WT_VERSION}/git-wt_${GIT_WT_VERSION}-1_${TARGETARCH}.deb" \
      -o /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && dpkg -i /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && rm /tmp/git-wt.deb

# Non-root user
RUN useradd -m -s /bin/bash agent
COPY docker/profile.sh /home/agent/.profile.sh
RUN echo '. ~/.profile.sh' >> /home/agent/.bashrc
USER agent
WORKDIR /home/agent

# mise
RUN curl -fsSL https://mise.run | sh
ENV PATH="/home/agent/.local/bin:${PATH}"

# Claude Code
RUN curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash

# entire (AI session capture for git)
RUN curl -fsSL https://entire.io/install.sh | bash

# Codex
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) CODEX_ARCH="x86_64" ;; \
      arm64) CODEX_ARCH="aarch64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/openai/codex/releases/download/rust-v${CODEX_VERSION}/codex-${CODEX_ARCH}-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz" \
      -o /tmp/codex.tar.gz \
    && tar xzf /tmp/codex.tar.gz -C /home/agent/.local/bin \
    && mv /home/agent/.local/bin/codex-*-unknown-linux-musl /home/agent/.local/bin/codex \
    && rm /tmp/codex.tar.gz

# APM (requires python3)
USER root
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3 python3-pip \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
USER agent
RUN pip3 install --user --break-system-packages apm-cli

WORKDIR /workspace
CMD ["sleep", "infinity"]

message.content: そうした場合 profile.sh とかどうする？

content.text:
`profile.sh` は bash でも zsh でも source できるので、今の構成で問題ないです:

- **zsh** (ユーザー): `.zshrc` → `. ~/.profile.sh`
- **bash** (agent): `.bashrc` → `. ~/.profile.sh`

どちらも同じ `profile.sh` を読むので、git-wt の init と Chrome 接続関数が両方で使えます。

ただし `eval "$(git wt --init bash)"` は zsh では `eval "$(git wt --init zsh)"` にする必要がありますね。

file.content:
eval "$(git wt --init bash)"

HOST_IP=$(getent hosts host.docker.internal 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}')
if [ -n "$HOST_IP" ]; then
  export CHROME_CDP_URL="http://${HOST_IP}:${CHROME_REMOTE_PORT:-9222}"
fi

chrome-connect() {
  if [ -z "$CHROME_CDP_URL" ]; then
    echo "CHROME_CDP_URL is not set" >&2
    return 1
  fi
  local ip="${CHROME_CDP_URL#http://}"
  local ws_url
  ws_url=$(curl -sf -H "Host: localhost" "$CHROME_CDP_URL/json/version" \
    | jq -r .webSocketDebuggerUrl \
    | sed "s|ws://localhost|ws://${ip}|")
  if [ -n "$ws_url" ] && [ "$ws_url" != "null" ]; then
    export CHROME_WS_URL="$ws_url"
    agent-browser connect "$CHROME_WS_URL"
  else
    echo "Failed to connect to Chrome at $CHROME_CDP_URL" >&2
    return 1
  fi
}

toolUseResult.originalFile:
eval "$(git wt --init bash)"

HOST_IP=$(getent hosts host.docker.internal 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}')
if [ -n "$HOST_IP" ]; then
  export CHROME_CDP_URL="http://${HOST_IP}:${CHROME_REMOTE_PORT:-9222}"
fi

chrome-connect() {
  if [ -z "$CHROME_CDP_URL" ]; then
    echo "CHROME_CDP_URL is not set" >&2
    return 1
  fi
  local ip="${CHROME_CDP_URL#http://}"
  local ws_url
  ws_url=$(curl -sf -H "Host: localhost" "$CHROME_CDP_URL/json/version" \
    | jq -r .webSocketDebuggerUrl \
    | sed "s|ws://localhost|ws://${ip}|")
  if [ -n "$ws_url" ] && [ "$ws_url" != "null" ]; then
    export CHROME_WS_URL="$ws_url"
    agent-browser connect "$CHROME_WS_URL"
  else
    echo "Failed to connect to Chrome at $CHROME_CDP_URL" >&2
    return 1
  fi
}

content.text: `$ZSH_VERSION` が設定されていれば `zsh`、なければ `bash` を渡します。これで profile.sh を bash/zsh 両方から共用できます。

message.content: agent もデフォルト zsh を使う？

content.text:
agent（Claude Code / Codex）は内部で `bash` や `sh` を使うので、ユーザーのデフォルトシェルが zsh でも影響ないです。

`useradd -s /bin/zsh` にしておけば `docker exec` で入ったときに zsh になるだけで、agent の動作には関係ありません。zsh で統一して問題ないです。

toolUseResult.originalFile:
FROM debian:trixie-slim

ARG RTK_VERSION=0.42.4
ARG CODEX_VERSION=0.141.0
ARG GIT_WT_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG TARGETARCH

ENV LANG=C.UTF-8
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# System packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    ca-certificates \
    curl \
    git \
    grep \
    jq \
    locales \
    openssh-client \
    ripgrep \
    tree \
    unzip \
    zsh \
    zsh-autosuggestions \
    zsh-syntax-highlighting \
    && sed -i 's/^# *\(C.UTF-8\)/\1/' /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# GitHub CLI
RUN curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
      -o /usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list \
    && apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends gh \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# RTK
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) RTK_PKG="rtk_${RTK_VERSION}-1_amd64.deb" ;; \
      arm64) RTK_PKG="rtk-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk/releases/download/v${RTK_VERSION}/${RTK_PKG}" -o "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" \
    && if [ "${TARGETARCH}" = "amd64" ]; then \
         dpkg -i "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"; \
       else \
         tar xzf "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" -C /usr/local/bin; \
       fi \
    && rm -f "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"

# agent-browser
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) AB_ARCH="x64" ;; \
      arm64) AB_ARCH="arm64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser/releases/download/v${AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION}/agent-browser-linux-${AB_ARCH}" \
      -o /usr/local/bin/agent-browser \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/agent-browser

# git-wt
RUN curl -fsSL "https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt/releases/download/v${GIT_WT_VERSION}/git-wt_${GIT_WT_VERSION}-1_${TARGETARCH}.deb" \
      -o /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && dpkg -i /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && rm /tmp/git-wt.deb

# Non-root user
RUN useradd -m -s /bin/zsh agent
COPY docker/profile.sh /home/agent/.profile.sh
COPY docker/.zshrc /home/agent/.zshrc
RUN echo '. ~/.profile.sh' >> /home/agent/.bashrc
USER agent
WORKDIR /home/agent

# mise
RUN curl -fsSL https://mise.run | sh
ENV PATH="/home/agent/.local/bin:${PATH}"

# Claude Code
RUN curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash

# entire (AI session capture for git)
RUN curl -fsSL https://entire.io/install.sh | bash

# Codex
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) CODEX_ARCH="x86_64" ;; \
      arm64) CODEX_ARCH="aarch64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/openai/codex/releases/download/rust-v${CODEX_VERSION}/codex-${CODEX_ARCH}-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz" \
      -o /tmp/codex.tar.gz \
    && tar xzf /tmp/codex.tar.gz -C /home/agent/.local/bin \
    && mv /home/agent/.local/bin/codex-*-unknown-linux-musl /home/agent/.local/bin/codex \
    && rm /tmp/codex.tar.gz

# APM (requires python3)
USER root
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3 python3-pip \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
USER agent
RUN pip3 install --user --break-system-packages apm-cli

WORKDIR /workspace
CMD ["sleep", "infinity"]

message.content: make file の claude とか zsh 経由しないから、chrome-connect できないんじゃない？ もしかしたら entrypoint で zsh とかいる？

content.text:
いい指摘です。`docker compose exec` は entrypoint を経由しないので、entrypoint を復活させても解決しません。

根本的な解決は `chrome-connect` をシェル関数ではなく **PATH 上のスクリプト** にすることです。そうすれば shell 初期化に依存せず、claude/codex の内部からも呼べます。

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
HOST_IP=$(getent hosts host.docker.internal 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}')
if [ -z "$HOST_IP" ]; then
  echo "host.docker.internal not found" >&2
  exit 1
fi

CHROME_PORT="${CHROME_REMOTE_PORT:-9222}"
CDP_URL="http://${HOST_IP}:${CHROME_PORT}"

WS_URL=$(curl -sf -H "Host: localhost" "$CDP_URL/json/version" \
  | jq -r .webSocketDebuggerUrl \
  | sed "s|ws://localhost|ws://${HOST_IP}:${CHROME_PORT}|")

if [ -n "$WS_URL" ] && [ "$WS_URL" != "null" ]; then
  echo "CHROME_WS_URL=$WS_URL"
  exec agent-browser connect "$WS_URL"
else
  echo "Failed to connect to Chrome at $CDP_URL" >&2
  exit 1
fi

toolUseResult.originalFile:
eval "$(git wt --init "${ZSH_VERSION:+zsh}" "${ZSH_VERSION:-bash}")"

HOST_IP=$(getent hosts host.docker.internal 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1}')
if [ -n "$HOST_IP" ]; then
  export CHROME_CDP_URL="http://${HOST_IP}:${CHROME_REMOTE_PORT:-9222}"
fi

chrome-connect() {
  if [ -z "$CHROME_CDP_URL" ]; then
    echo "CHROME_CDP_URL is not set" >&2
    return 1
  fi
  local ip="${CHROME_CDP_URL#http://}"
  local ws_url
  ws_url=$(curl -sf -H "Host: localhost" "$CHROME_CDP_URL/json/version" \
    | jq -r .webSocketDebuggerUrl \
    | sed "s|ws://localhost|ws://${ip}|")
  if [ -n "$ws_url" ] && [ "$ws_url" != "null" ]; then
    export CHROME_WS_URL="$ws_url"
    agent-browser connect "$CHROME_WS_URL"
  else
    echo "Failed to connect to Chrome at $CHROME_CDP_URL" >&2
    return 1
  fi
}

message.content: agent container から 他のcontainer の app test などを叩きたいときって、host os から docker socket を共有するひつようがある？

lastPrompt: agent container から 他のcontainer の app test などを叩きたいときって、host os から docker socket を共有するひつようがある？

aiTitle: AI Docker container with MCP and agent-browser setup

content.text:
はい、コンテナ内から `docker` コマンドで他のコンテナを操作するには Docker socket の共有が必要です。

compose.yml で:

```yaml
volumes:
  - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
```

ただしこれはホストの Docker を完全に制御できるので、セキュリティリスクがあります。agent がコンテナを消したり作ったりできてしまいます。

用途としては:
- **他のコンテナの API を叩くだけ** → socket 不要。Docker network を共有すれば `http://app:3000` で直接アクセスできる
- **docker compose up/down したい** → socket が必要
- **docker exec したい** → socket が必要

テスト対象の app に HTTP でアクセスするだけなら、同じ Docker network に参加させるだけで済みます。どちらのパターンですか？

message.content: contaienr に対して、exec などをしたい。 なぜならvolume sync で共有されるproject 内の make に docker compose run --rm app bin/rails db:prepare などがかいてあり、それを実行する必要があるため

FROM debian:trixie-slim

ARG RTK_VERSION=0.42.4
ARG CODEX_VERSION=0.141.0
ARG GIT_WT_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION=0.29.0
ARG TARGETARCH

ENV LANG=C.UTF-8
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# System packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    ca-certificates \
    curl \
    git \
    grep \
    jq \
    locales \
    openssh-client \
    ripgrep \
    tree \
    unzip \
    zsh \
    zsh-autosuggestions \
    zsh-syntax-highlighting \
    && sed -i 's/^# *\(C.UTF-8\)/\1/' /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# GitHub CLI
RUN curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
      -o /usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
    && echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main" \
      > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list \
    && apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends gh \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# starship prompt
RUN curl -fsSL https://starship.rs/install.sh | sh -s -- -y

# RTK
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) RTK_PKG="rtk_${RTK_VERSION}-1_amd64.deb" ;; \
      arm64) RTK_PKG="rtk-aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk/releases/download/v${RTK_VERSION}/${RTK_PKG}" -o "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" \
    && if [ "${TARGETARCH}" = "amd64" ]; then \
         dpkg -i "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"; \
       else \
         tar xzf "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}" -C /usr/local/bin; \
       fi \
    && rm -f "/tmp/${RTK_PKG}"

# agent-browser
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) AB_ARCH="x64" ;; \
      arm64) AB_ARCH="arm64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser/releases/download/v${AGENT_BROWSER_VERSION}/agent-browser-linux-${AB_ARCH}" \
      -o /usr/local/bin/agent-browser \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/agent-browser

# git-wt
RUN curl -fsSL "https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt/releases/download/v${GIT_WT_VERSION}/git-wt_${GIT_WT_VERSION}-1_${TARGETARCH}.deb" \
      -o /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && dpkg -i /tmp/git-wt.deb \
    && rm /tmp/git-wt.deb

# Scripts
COPY docker/bin/chrome-connect /usr/local/bin/chrome-connect

# Non-root user
RUN useradd -m -s /bin/zsh agent
COPY docker/profile.sh /home/agent/.profile.sh
COPY docker/.zshrc /home/agent/.zshrc
RUN echo '. ~/.profile.sh' >> /home/agent/.bashrc
USER agent
WORKDIR /home/agent

# mise
RUN curl -fsSL https://mise.run | sh
ENV PATH="/home/agent/.local/bin:${PATH}"

# Claude Code
RUN curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash

# entire (AI session capture for git)
RUN curl -fsSL https://entire.io/install.sh | bash

# Codex
RUN case "${TARGETARCH}" in \
      amd64) CODEX_ARCH="x86_64" ;; \
      arm64) CODEX_ARCH="aarch64" ;; \
      *) echo "Unsupported arch: ${TARGETARCH}" && exit 1 ;; \
    esac \
    && curl -fsSL "https://github.com/openai/codex/releases/download/rust-v${CODEX_VERSION}/codex-${CODEX_ARCH}-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz" \
      -o /tmp/codex.tar.gz \
    && tar xzf /tmp/codex.tar.gz -C /home/agent/.local/bin \
    && mv /home/agent/.local/bin/codex-*-unknown-linux-musl /home/agent/.local/bin/codex \
    && rm /tmp/codex.tar.gz

# APM (requires python3)
USER root
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3 python3-pip \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
USER agent
RUN pip3 install --user --break-system-packages apm-cli

WORKDIR /workspace
CMD ["sleep", "infinity"]

# agent-stack

AI agent development container with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code), [Codex](https://github.com/openai/codex), [RTK](https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk), [mise](https://mise.jdx.dev/), [APM](https://github.com/microsoft/apm), [entire](https://entire.io), and [git-wt](https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt) pre-installed.

## Quick Start

Add to your project's `compose.yml`:

```yaml
services:
  agent:
    image: ghcr.io/syati/agent-stack:latest
    volumes:
      - .:/workspace
    env_file:
      - .env
    extra_hosts:
      - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway"
```

Start the container and connect:

```bash
docker compose up -d
docker compose exec -it agent bash
```

Use `docker compose exec` (not `docker attach`). Each `exec` spawns an independent process, so multiple agents can run in parallel without stdin conflicts.

## What's Inside

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) | Anthropic's AI coding CLI |
| [Codex](https://github.com/openai/codex) | OpenAI's AI coding CLI |
| [RTK](https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk) | Token-optimized CLI proxy (60-90% token savings) |
| [mise](https://mise.jdx.dev/) | Dev tool version manager |
| [APM](https://github.com/microsoft/apm) | Agent Package Manager for MCP/skills |
| [entire](https://entire.io) | AI session capture for git |
| [git-wt](https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt) | Simplified git worktree management |
| [gh](https://cli.github.com/) | GitHub CLI |
| [ripgrep](https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep) | Fast grep (auto-used by RTK) |
| [agent-browser](https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser) | Browser automation for AI agents |
| build-essential | C/C++ compiler toolchain |

## Environment Variables

Create a `.env` file:

```
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_...
```

## Local Build

See [compose.yml](compose.yml) for a working example.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Syati/agent-stack.git
cd agent-stack
cp .env.example .env
# edit .env with your keys

make build   # build image
make dev     # start container
make shell   # open shell
make claude  # run Claude Code
make codex   # run Codex
make clean   # stop and remove
```

## agent-browser Integration

file.content:
# agent-stack

AI agent development container with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code), [Codex](https://github.com/openai/codex), [RTK](https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk), [mise](https://mise.jdx.dev/), [APM](https://github.com/microsoft/apm), [entire](https://entire.io), and [git-wt](https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt) pre-installed.

## Quick Start

Add to your project's `compose.yml`:

```yaml
services:
  agent:
    image: ghcr.io/syati/agent-stack:latest
    volumes:
      - .:/workspace
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock  # host Docker access
    env_file:
      - .env
    extra_hosts:
      - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway"
```

Docker socket をマウントすると、コンテナ内からホストの Docker を操作できます（`docker compose run`, `docker exec` 等）。不要な場合は socket の行を削除してください。

Start the container and connect:

```bash
docker compose up -d
docker compose exec -it agent bash
```

Use `docker compose exec` (not `docker attach`). Each `exec` spawns an independent process, so multiple agents can run in parallel without stdin conflicts.

## What's Inside

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) | Anthropic's AI coding CLI |
| [Codex](https://github.com/openai/codex) | OpenAI's AI coding CLI |
| [RTK](https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk) | Token-optimized CLI proxy (60-90% token savings) |
| [mise](https://mise.jdx.dev/) | Dev tool version manager |
| [APM](https://github.com/microsoft/apm) | Agent Package Manager for MCP/skills |
| [entire](https://entire.io) | AI session capture for git |
| [git-wt](https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt) | Simplified git worktree management |
| [gh](https://cli.github.com/) | GitHub CLI |
| [ripgrep](https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep) | Fast grep (auto-used by RTK) |
| [agent-browser](https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser) | Browser automation for AI agents |
| build-essential | C/C++ compiler toolchain |

## Environment Variables

Create a `.env` file:

```
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_...
```

## Local Build

See [compose.yml](compose.yml) for a working example.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Syati/agent-stack.git
cd agent-stack
cp .env.example .env
# edit .env with your keys

make build   # build image
make dev     # start container
make shell   # open shell
make claude  # run Claude Code
make codex   # run Codex
make clean   # stop and remove
```

## agent-browser Integration

[agent-browser](https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser) is pre-installed in the container. Start Chrome on the host with remote debugging, then connect from the container.

**Host side** (`make chrome` or manually):

```bash
/Applications/Google\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\ Chrome \
  --remote-debugging-port=9222 \
  --remote-debugging-address=0.0.0.0 \
  --user-data-dir=$HOME/.chrome-agent \
  --no-first-run \
  --no-default-browser-check \
  --password-store=basic \
  --disable-blink-features=AutomationControlled
```

**Container side** (run `chrome-connect` to resolve WebSocket URL and connect):

```bash
chrome-connect
```

Port is configurable via `CHROME_REMOTE_PORT` in `.env` (default: 9222).

## License

MIT

content.text:
消すとコンテナを再作成するたびに Claude Code / Codex のセッション履歴や mise でインストールした言語ランタイムが消えます。

ただ、使い捨ての環境として割り切るなら不要ですね。セッション履歴は `entire` が git に記録しているし、mise のランタイムは再インストールすればいいので。

どうしますか？

questions.question: named volume を残しますか？

options.label: 残す
options.description: コンテナ再作成でもセッション履歴・mise ランタイムを保持

options.label: 消す
options.description: 使い捨て前提。毎回クリーンな状態で起動

content.content: Your questions have been answered: "named volume を残しますか？"="exec でもきえるよね？". You can now continue with these answers in mind.

questions.question: named volume を残しますか？

options.label: 残す
options.description: コンテナ再作成でもセッション履歴・mise ランタイムを保持

options.label: 消す
options.description: 使い捨て前提。毎回クリーンな状態で起動

content.text:
いえ、`docker exec` では消えません。named volume が消えるのは `docker compose down -v` のときだけです。

- `docker exec` → 新しいプロセスを追加するだけ、何も消えない
- `docker compose restart` → 消えない
- `docker compose down` → コンテナ削除、volume は残る
- `docker compose down -v` → volume も消える

なので named volume があれば、`make clean`（`down -v`）しない限りデータは永続します。残しておく方が便利だと思います。

# agent-stack

AI agent development container with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code), [Codex](https://github.com/openai/codex), [RTK](https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk), [mise](https://mise.jdx.dev/), [APM](https://github.com/microsoft/apm), [entire](https://entire.io), and [git-wt](https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt) pre-installed.

## Quick Start

Add to your project's `compose.yml`:

```yaml
services:
  agent:
    image: ghcr.io/syati/agent-stack:latest
    volumes:
      - .:/workspace
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock  # host Docker access
    env_file:
      - .env
    extra_hosts:
      - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway"
```

Mounting the Docker socket allows the container to control host Docker (`docker compose run`, `docker exec`, etc.). Remove the socket line if not needed.

Start the container and connect:

```bash
docker compose up -d
docker compose exec -it agent bash
```

Use `docker compose exec` (not `docker attach`). Each `exec` spawns an independent process, so multiple agents can run in parallel without stdin conflicts.

## Container User

Runs as non-root user `agent` (home: `/home/agent`, shell: `zsh`). Working directory is `/workspace`.

## What's Inside

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) | Anthropic's AI coding CLI |
| [Codex](https://github.com/openai/codex) | OpenAI's AI coding CLI |
| [RTK](https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk) | Token-optimized CLI proxy (60-90% token savings) |
| [mise](https://mise.jdx.dev/) | Dev tool version manager |
| [APM](https://github.com/microsoft/apm) | Agent Package Manager for MCP/skills |
| [entire](https://entire.io) | AI session capture for git |
| [git-wt](https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt) | Simplified git worktree management |
| [gh](https://cli.github.com/) | GitHub CLI |
| [ripgrep](https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep) | Fast grep (auto-used by RTK) |
| [agent-browser](https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser) | Browser automation for AI agents |
| build-essential | C/C++ compiler toolchain |

## Environment Variables

Create a `.env` file:

```
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_...
```

## Local Build

See [compose.yml](compose.yml) for a working example.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Syati/agent-stack.git
cd agent-stack
cp .env.example .env
# edit .env with your keys

make build   # build image
make dev     # start container
make shell   # open shell
make claude  # run Claude Code
make codex   # run Codex
make clean   # stop and remove
```

## agent-browser Integration

[agent-browser](https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser) is pre-installed in the container. Start Chrome on the host with remote debugging, then connect from the container.

**Host side** (`make chrome` or manually):

```bash
/Applications/Google\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\ Chrome \
  --remote-debugging-port=9222 \
  --remote-debugging-address=0.0.0.0 \
  --user-data-dir=$HOME/.chrome-agent \
  --no-first-run \
  --no-default-browser-check \
  --password-store=basic \
  --disable-blink-features=AutomationControlled
```

**Container side** (run `chrome-connect` to resolve WebSocket URL and connect):

```bash
chrome-connect
```

Port is configurable via `CHROME_REMOTE_PORT` in `.env` (default: 9222).

## License

MIT

addedLines: - entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# agent-stack

AI agent development container with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code), [Codex](https://github.com/openai/codex), [RTK](https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk), [mise](https://mise.jdx.dev/), [APM](https://github.com/microsoft/apm), [entire](https://entire.io), and [git-wt](https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt) pre-installed.

## Quick Start

Add to your project's `compose.yml`:

```yaml
services:
  agent:
    image: ghcr.io/syati/agent-stack:latest
    volumes:
      - .:/workspace
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock  # host Docker access
      - claude-home:/home/agent/.claude
      - codex-home:/home/agent/.codex
      - mise-data:/home/agent/.local/share/mise
    env_file:
      - .env
    extra_hosts:
      - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway"

volumes:
  claude-home:
  codex-home:
  mise-data:
```

- **Docker socket**: allows the container to control host Docker (`docker compose run`, `docker exec`, etc.). Remove if not needed.
- **Named volumes**: persist Claude Code/Codex session history and mise-installed runtimes across container recreations. Only cleared by `docker compose down -v`.

Start the container and connect:

```bash
docker compose up -d
docker compose exec -it agent bash
```

Use `docker compose exec` (not `docker attach`). Each `exec` spawns an independent process, so multiple agents can run in parallel without stdin conflicts.

## Container User

Runs as non-root user `agent` (home: `/home/agent`, shell: `zsh`). Working directory is `/workspace`.

## What's Inside

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) | Anthropic's AI coding CLI |
| [Codex](https://github.com/openai/codex) | OpenAI's AI coding CLI |
| [RTK](https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk) | Token-optimized CLI proxy (60-90% token savings) |
| [mise](https://mise.jdx.dev/) | Dev tool version manager |
| [APM](https://github.com/microsoft/apm) | Agent Package Manager for MCP/skills |
| [entire](https://entire.io) | AI session capture for git |
| [git-wt](https://github.com/k1LoW/git-wt) | Simplified git worktree management |
| [gh](https://cli.github.com/) | GitHub CLI |
| [ripgrep](https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep) | Fast grep (auto-used by RTK) |
| [agent-browser](https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser) | Browser automation for AI agents |
| build-essential | C/C++ compiler toolchain |

## Environment Variables

Create a `.env` file:

```
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_...
```

## Local Build

See [compose.yml](compose.yml) for a working example.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Syati/agent-stack.git
cd agent-stack
cp .env.example .env
# edit .env with your keys

make build   # build image
make dev     # start container
make shell   # open shell
make claude  # run Claude Code
make codex   # run Codex
make clean   # stop and remove
```

## agent-browser Integration

[agent-browser](https://github.com/vercel-labs/agent-browser) is pre-installed in the container. Start Chrome on the host with remote debugging, then connect from the container.

**Host side** (`make chrome` or manually):

```bash
/Applications/Google\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\ Chrome \
  --remote-debugging-port=9222 \
  --remote-debugging-address=0.0.0.0 \
  --user-data-dir=$HOME/.chrome-agent \
  --no-first-run \
  --no-default-browser-check \
  --password-store=basic \
  --disable-blink-features=AutomationControlled
```

**Container side** (run `chrome-connect` to resolve WebSocket URL and connect):

```bash
chrome-connect
```

Port is configurable via `CHROME_REMOTE_PORT` in `.env` (default: 9222).

## License

MIT
